compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect shared hook error handler and new log path
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Updates existing project documentation to reflect the new shared hook error handler architecture and canonical log path.

**Why**: After the epic completes, CLAUDE.md will reference an outdated log path and agents reading architecture docs will be unaware of the new handler pattern. Stale docs lead to inconsistent future hook implementations.

**Scope**:
- IN: `CLAUDE.md` — update any references from `hook-error-log.jsonl` to `dso-hook-errors.jsonl`; add note about `hook-error-handler.sh` pattern for new hook authors; update `hooks/lib/` inventory if listed; update enforcement hook boundary convention note. `KNOWN-ISSUES.md` — update any entries referencing old log path. Any other existing docs that reference the legacy path.
- OUT: Creating new documentation files; updating inline hook-script comments (handled in S2–S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, no reference to `~/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl` (legacy path) remains in CLAUDE.md, KNOWN-ISSUES.md, or other project docs
  ← Satisfies: "Legacy path deprecated; all readers and test fixtures updated to new path"
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md documents the `_dso_hook_err_handler` pattern and `# hook-boundary: enforcement` convention for future hook authors
  ← Satisfies: "Enforcement hooks explicitly excluded with machine-enforced boundary (header convention + pre-commit check)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all modified documentation (structural boundary tests only — does not test content)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
